name: US FedRAMP Moderate / StateRAMP public-sector digital service

rules.description: Centralized tamper-evident audit pipeline meeting NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5 AU-2 / AU-12 with 3-year retention.
FIPS 140-3 validated cryptography on all data-plane TLS; CMK for storage holding PII; key rotation cadence explicit.
Account management with periodic recertification; least privilege; CAC / PIV-aware MFA for privileged actions; break-glass documented.
Subprocessor inventory with FedRAMP authorization status documented; vendor risk reviews before production connectivity.
Network segmentation with deny-by-default east-west traffic; private endpoints for SQL / Blob; no public data-plane egress; ATO boundary documented in manifest.
Backup, restore, and RTO/RPO tested quarterly; failover targets must remain in Azure Government regions only — no commercial-cloud failover for citizen data.
Change windows with documented approval; automated drift detection for production configs; configuration baselines (CM-2) versioned alongside the manifest.
Continuous monitoring (ConMon) telemetry feed to agency SOC; alerting on privileged actions and data-exfiltration patterns; vulnerability scans monthly with 30-day high-severity remediation SLA.
